content: GABA is a neurotransmitter that acts as an inhibitory neurotransmitter, turning neurons off.
A study found that 800 milligrams of GABA taken 30 to 60 minutes on an empty stomach improved decision-making and quick adaptation to changing rules in an experimental game.
Taking GABA can help focus attention by shutting off distractions, aiding in making quick decisions and reacting quickly.
GABA may cross the blood-brain barrier, affecting brain function, though the mechanism is unclear.
To try GABA, start with 800 milligrams 30 to 60 minutes before it matters, and monitor for effects and duration.
